entry.title: Re: Mask in CJ20N - "Screen" field
entry.content:
Do you see the "Project Coding Mask" with the Mask ID underneath it?

The mask itself will show if you enter a project id that has a configured mask.  In your screen shot the project id is blank.

entry.title: Re: upgrading SAP BW on non-HANA database but S/4 as source
entry.content:
if your source-system is at least one of these releases ...
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BW_BRIDGE/107a6e8a38b74ede94c833ca3b7b6f51/327833022dcf42159a5bec552663dc51.html?locale=en-US
... then you can use BW bridge or load data via the ODP extractors

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere and SAP Data Intelligence Cloud - what does this mean for me now?
entry.content: Will Datasphere have data quality monitoring capabilities?

entry.title: SAP CUSTOMER CHECKOUT MANAGER ARTICLES UPDATE FROM SAP B1
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are using SAP Customer Checkout Version CCO_2.0_FP13_SP05 with SAP Business One 10.0 (10.00.191) FP 2202 as backend system.

The synchronisation of items from B1 to CCO is ok, but when we check the sales reports in SAP Customer Checkout Manager there are incoherences between items codes and name for example in the report we have item 'A0001' with description 'A' but in SAP B1 item 'A0001' have description 'B'.

So we checked the "articles" tiles in SAP Customer Checkout Manager the total of items is not the same as SAP B1 and some items have not the right description.

My question is where do the items in SAP Customer Checkout Manager are from ? and is it possible to update them in mass to match the items in SAP B1 ?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Studio : standalone or eclipse plugin
entry.content: ... this is only possible if you have modern SAP-cloud-version (for BAS) and a separate server with HANA cockpit installed

The link into the Cdertification is not valid, the url doesn´t exist!

 

Thank you all.

Carol.

entry.title: Re: SAP Material management Consultant level Certification

entry.title: Smart Forms not printing on Zebra ZT231 Printer
entry.content:
I've created a Smart Form for a 1.125 X 3.5 label to be sent to a Zebra ZT231.  When printing to the ZT231 printer as the Windows default printer it works fine.  When we try to print directly to the printer on the network using the ZLZEB2 device type we get some data output, but not in the format of the Smart Form. After researching OSS notes on the issues we are having we found that the label needed to be designed to the size specifications of the label and use one of the YZB300 device types.

We have since formatted the page size for the label to 29mm X 89mm (couldn't use inches with decimals) and are using the YZB300U device type.  When we try to print directly to the printer from SAP no spool is being created and it will not print on the printer.

What I've done so far:

  • Created new device type based on YZB300U
  • Created new portrait and landscape page formats 
  • Created new format type for new page format
  • Changed Smart Form page format to the newly created page format
  • Changed label orientation to landscape
  • Changed the printer device type to the newly created device type

Can anyone tell me what I'm missing to get this working?

entry.title: Re: I want to have an app like CNMASSCREATE in S4 HANA Pr...
entry.content: CNMASSCREATE still works in S/4HANA Private Cloud

entry.title: VS Code SAP FIORI Tools - Application Modeler
entry.content: Recently VS Code and SAP FIORI tools extension was updated and now Application Generator command does not work.  It doesn't do anything.

entry.title: New Member in the SAP Learning Community
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My name is Lucía Melián, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I’m an Industrial Organization Engineer with six years of experience in the logistics field. Over the past three years, I’ve worked as a Project Manager on a logistics project, where I discovered my ability to understand client needs and help them achieve their goals.

In recent years, I have developed a deep interest in SAP solutions. In 2024, I completed a Master’s in SAP Logistics Project Management, gaining a solid understanding of the MM and SD modules, along with their respective certifications.

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in cloud computing, supply chain management and logistics optimization.

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because it aligns with my goal of achieving my first opportunity as an SAP Consultant, equipping myself with key tools such as digital skills. I recognize the growing trend of companies migrating from on-premise infrastructures to cloud platforms, along with their emphasis on digital transformation. Becoming SAP certified will not only allow me to stay at the forefront of technological innovations but also enable me to add greater value to the logistics projects I will be involved in.

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in SAP Logistics solutions, collaborating on projects, or discussing the latest trends in digital transformation.

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

Best regards,  

Lucía Melián

entry.title: Re: 🔵 Integrating Generative AI in SAP S/4HANA with ISLM
entry.content: Where to find validation page related to this presentation? I don't see questions.

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because it aligns with my goal of achieving my first opportunity as an SAP Consultant, equipping myself with key tools such as digital skills. Becoming SAP certified will not only allow me to stay at the forefront of technological innovations but also enable me to add greater value to the logistics projects I will be involved in.

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in SAP Logistics solutions, collaborating on projects, or discussing the latest trends in digital transformation.

Best regards,

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch in Leipzig
entry.content:
Liebe SAP Hamburg-Community!

Wir sind am 14. Oktober 2024 anlässlich des DSAG-Kongresses in Leipzig . Alle die ebenfalls vor Ort sind laden wir herzlich zu einem Stammtisch in Leipzig ein!

Wir freuen uns auf euch!

entry.title: View existing customer user-exit of SAP CRM - JAVA IPC pricing routine in Eclipse ADT
entry.content:
Introduction: One business functionality in SAP CRM billing required development on SAP CRM JAVA pricing customer user-exit. I was wondering how to see the running code in an existing user exit. In order to address this, we must first download it using the T-code /SAPCND/UE_DEV from the system and choose the Userexits option, providing the package name and Target Directory. 

The ABAP Package name corresponds to the Module name from SM53 under the Installation -> Components -> /0SAP/IPC -> Modules -> Files. Take a cue from there, but omit SAPCND_UE at the end and /0CUST/ at the beginning. In this case, ZZCRM_P0 would be the value.

Choose Target Directory in the local system to download the related files. 

Next we would be able to see a new folder named userexits in the target directory. 

And now we can check the code in Eclipse ADT as per below by opening package explorer PRC_UE_CUSTOMER.

I truly hope you found this stuff interesting to read. For further reading, please see SAP Note 809820 - Userexit concept for pricing. I would appreciate your suggestions and opinions at any time. And many thanks again for your valuable time.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, October 3rd, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/10/03/sap-developer-news-october-3rd-2024/

DESCRIPTION

UI5 linter 1.0 and SAPUI5 Roadmap

  • Release of UI5 linter 1.0 https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/release-of-ui5-linter-v1-0/ba-p/13880556
  • SAPUI5 Roadmap on SAP Roadmap Explorer https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800001361&range=CURRENT-LAST#Q4%202024

Devtoberfest Week 2 Recap

  • Devtoberfest Homepage: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Contest Week 2: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-week-2-contest-activities/ba-p/13880856
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d
  • Devtoberfest will be on pause next week for SAP TechEd Virtual. It will resume on October 14th.

SAP TechEd Virtual

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP Community Welcome: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1723237330354001PDnG

What's New in SAP HANA Cloud 2024/Q3

  • What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – September 2024: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-hana-cloud-september-2024/ba-p/13873862
  • New Machine Learning features in SAP HANA Cloud 2024 Q3: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/new-machine-learning-features-in-sap-hana-cloud-2024-q3/ba-p/13874878
  • Calculation View Features of 2024 QRC3: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/calculation-view-features-of-2024-qrc3/ba-p/13866974
  • What's New in SAP HANA Cloud Central | SAP Help Portal https://help.sap.com/whats-new/2495b34492334456a49084831c2bea4e?locale=en-US&Valid_as_Of=2024-09-01:2024-09-30
  •  Where can I acquire the skills to use monitor and performance tools within SAP HANA Cockpit?: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-learning-blog-posts/where-can-i-acquire-the-skills-to-use-monitor-and-performance-tools-within/ba-p/13874201
  • What's New with SAP Datasphere in Q3, 2024? https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-community/what-s-new-with-sap-datasphere-in-q3-2024/ec-p/13808661#M638

SAP Partner ABAP Cloud Partner Certification Academy

  • Academy Details (Partner Portal Access Required):  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2024/jan/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00003900_Webinar_English.html?#accordion-panel-section-libraryItem-descriptionSection-accordioniteml

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 UI5 linter 1.0
1:47 SAPUI5 Roadmap
2:22 Devtoberfest Week 2 Recap
4:31 SAP TechEd Virtual
5:20 What's New in SAP HANA Cloud 2024/Q3
7:33 SAP Partner ABAP Cloud Partner Certification Academy

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for October 3rd, 2024.

[DJ] In any software project, there are best practices to which, ideally, developers will adhere. I am talking about things like code readability and maintainability, code standards and style guidelines, quality and security. Now, a linter is the ideal tool for checking for and gently but only enforcing these best practices. Now, back in March of this year, the UI5 linter was announced in a blog post from Oliver. Now, the initial version still had lots of gaps, but it was warmly received by the developer community, who gave lots of feedback, which was in turn gratefully received and incorporated into the tool. Now, earlier this week saw the official 1.0 release of the UI5 linter. This 1.0 release adds more problem detections including use of deprecated framework components and APIs and also old style manifests and deprecated library usage. Now, of course, the linter even at 1.0 is still not quite complete, but the team are working hard on the items which they've put onto a roadmap. All it takes to get going with the UI5 linter is a quick npm install global of the UI5 linter package and you're off to the races.

By the way, talking of roadmaps, the latest SAPUI5 roadmap has been released. This roadmap covers the developments that are planned for the rest of this year, but also for the first two quarters of 2025. The roadmap covers items in the area of SAP Fiori development, UX consistency, UI controls and more. So head on over to the road map area to check it out.

[Tom] I can't believe we've already covered two full weeks of Devtoberfest. It's been great to see so many people joining the educational sessions, completing tutorials, tracking their progress on the game board, and overall interacting with each other on the community. Now, after just the first five days of Devtoberfest, we already had over 20,000 unique viewers on the content of Devtoberfest. That's a great chunk of the community coming out to enjoy all this Devtoberfest content. And over half of those, 20,000, had never watched any video on the SAP Developers YouTube channel. So maybe we have some new viewers here watching the developer news for the first time. If so, that's you, welcome and I hope you stick around and enjoy more news that we bring you every week on this channel.

Now Devtoberfest is taking a slight pause here. We had the first two weeks of Devtoberfest. Next week is SAP TechEd. We have the virtual TechEd taking place on Tuesday and Wednesday. The developer advocates will be live from Germany doing the developer keynote deconstructed as well as bringing you special episodes of the developer news on this channel at the end of each of those days. There we'll recap the big announcements and other highlights for the TechEd event. But don't worry, then the week after TechEd, we're going to return with more Devtoberfest. We have two full more weeks of Devtoberfest content coming your way, and in those two weeks we can particularly cover in detail some of the things that were just announced at TechEd, so you have plenty more time to continue on the Devtoberfest contest. Maybe next week gives you a little chance to catch up on some of the videos that you missed, in addition to tuning in to check out TechEd. So we look forward to seeing you for more weeks of Devtoberfest, as well as the TechEd virtual live online next week. And to tell you more about TechEd over to you, you, Josh.

[Josh] We are days away from coming to you live from the App House in Heidelberg. It's going to be unbelievable. 90 plus sessions, all the developer advocates from around the world, tons of customer and expert speakers all under one roof. But you've got to sign up now. We'll have live keynote analysis plus live Q&A where you can get answers to your questions in real time or interact with a specific session speaker. You'll miss this chance to take in code demos, live lectures, and virtual workshops all designed to help you deepen your technical expertise and, most importantly, interact and have fun with our incredible community of thinkers and creators. So what are you waiting for? Sign up now and I'll see you online next week.

[Witalij] I'd like to share with you my summary of the top 5 features introduced in the Q3 2024 release of SAP HANA Cloud. First, SAP HANA Cloud now supports vector indexing, which is crucial for efficiently managing and querying large vector datasets. This feature enables faster and more efficient searches, particularly useful for machine learning and AI workloads. The release includes the addition of the HDB scan clustering algorithm enhancing the capabilities of unsupervised machine learning. This enhancement enables data scientists and application developers using SAP HANA Cloud among you to capture clusters in heterogeneous data and identify groups of similar objects or text documents based on embeddings in vector data. Third, in the SAP HANA native application web space for SAP Business Application Studio, You can now use the generative AI tools included in SAP Build Code to help you create SQL code based on the prompt you type into the console using natural language. It simplifies query construction and reduces time required for developing complex data analysis queries. Fourth, in the SAP HANA native application dev space for SAP Business Application Studio, it is now possible to use the SQL Notebooks to test your SQL code on SAP HANA Cloud database before you run it productively. And last but not least, there are a lot of important new features in calculation views modeling. And I would recommend reviewing detailed description of all of them in a separate blog post written by product managers. All these features focus on enhancing the efficiency, flexibility, and productivity of data management and analysis within SAP HANA Cloud, offering more advanced tools for developers and data scientists. And if you are looking for what's new in SAP DataSphere in Q3, then product management prepared a separate community call for you to be streamed on October 16th.

[Tom] This specific new segment is targeted right at our SAP partners. We have a webinar series coming up to help partners who are looking to do their ABAP Cloud certification. This is the certification back end  developer ABAP Cloud. And in particular, this webinar series is going to help you with content around core ABAP skills, as well as practicing clean core extensibility for SAP S4HANA. Now, this webinar series is coming up to cover all the materials that I mentioned. It is a multi-day, in fact, four-day webinar series with three to three and a half hours of content each day. This is coming up at the end of October, October 22nd, 23rd, 24th, and October 29th. You're interested in this kind of content to help you as a partner developer who's looking to update their skills for about Cloud, then check out the link in the description to the Parker Edge portal page with more information about this webinar series.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
Re: Enable Now Producer will not create Documents from Sims
entry.content:
Hi David,

There are a couple of KBAs that can help you identify the issue.  I would start with the following:

3411718 - SAP Enable Now Producer: PDF export visible in library, but no PDF file is generated
2476196 - Troubleshooting when documents fail to generate

If these KBAs don't help, please raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-PRO and get support involved.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: Keeping it simple!

This was answered in YT comments: "Most of the features will be available with OP2025 latest. Some of them are already available with OP2023"

@MecsiMoto- also FYI

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
My name is Jose Luis Garcia and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I am from South Texas and have resided in Austin, TX for the past 5 years. I am currently a Court Clerk II with the District Clerk’s Office for Travis County. I enjoy working out, running, hiking, spending time with loved ones and exploring my city. 

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in SAP Training Business Analyst, Travis County uses a county-wide integrated system (SAP ERP) for finance, procurement and HCM. I am eager to learn about SAP and hopefully expand my career into a SAP Support team for Travis County. 

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because I would be able to expand my skill sets and make a leap into A technology career. As I previously mentioned my current employer uses a county-wide integrated system and I would like to make the transition from Court Clerk to a Business Analyst.   

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in AI and Cybersecurity. 

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

Best regards,  
Jose Luis Garcia

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in SAP Training Business Analyst, Travis County uses a county-wide integrated system (SAP ERP) for finance, procurement and HCM. I am eager to learn about SAP and hopefully expand my career into a SAP Support team for Travis County.

entry.title: Re: Stock overview in MM in Company code & Controlling Area currency
entry.content: How can we possibly do this to see both currencies. Please help

entry.title: Re: SAC Planning BPC live model - Publish data from the s...
entry.content:
Try this step. If you are executing all the planning sequence through process chain, at the end of the planning sequence, add a program to switch the ADSO from planning mode to loading mode and back to planning Mode. SAP note: 3455780 , you can use, class CL_RSPLS_CUBE_SWITCH_L and CL_RSPLS_CUBE_SWITCH_P

Thanks

Savio

entry.title: A bit about myself
entry.content:
My name is Jerry Sarpey, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I have a strong foundation in cybersecurity, holding certifications such as CompTIA Security+, Certified in Cybersecurity, Google Cybersecurity Certification, and CompTIA ITF, I am committed to safeguarding information assets. My experience as a Client Relationship Manager at Morgan Stanley has honed my skills in managing client needs and ensuring secure IT environments. With a robust background in both banking and warehousing, I bring a unique blend of financial acumen and logistical expertise to the table. This diverse skill set enables me to effectively bridge the gap between financial strategy and operational efficiency, driving comprehensive solutions that enhance organizational performance.

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in supply chain management and finance.

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because it will validate my skills and open me to new career opportunities in the rapidly growing field of SAP consulting. Overall, this certification is a crucial step in advancing my career and contributing to the success of any organization I work with.

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in cybersecurity, AI and banking

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

 

Best regards,  

Jerry Sarpey

entry.title: Re: upgrading SAP BW on non-HANA database but S/4 as source
entry.content:
1. It is possible to tech upgrade to SAP BW 7.5 on SQL while keeping your existing data sources from ECC6 intact. BW 7.5 supports legacy objects from BW 7.0, meaning you can maintain your current InfoProviders like InfoCubes, Multiproviders without converting them immediately.

2. You can interface with S/4HANA as a data source later on, but keep in mind that newer features and optimizations for S/4HANA may not be fully utilized if you use older InfoProviders.

3. The BW Bridge will work with both legacy and new objects, but using new objects like ADSOs and composite providers can provide good performance and flexible data modeling, which is beneficial when integrating with Datasphere.

Thanks

Savio

entry.title: Re: Fiori Element List Report - Untick checkbox after custom action
entry.content: I am having the same issue. Any solution?

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, 

Is there a separate blog for BEN: Secure Act 2.0 - Section 109? We have implemented the HRSPs, and a new note was released on September 15th—3511561 - BEN: Secure Act 2.0 - Section 109, which addresses the issue of not correctly considering employees who reach 63 years old. We implemented it, but it's not working as expected.

Has anyone else implemented this SAP note and received the expected results?

Thank you for your response. 

Regards,

Suneetha

entry.title: Re: CAP How to filter on function result
entry.content: Hello @yuliya_seliukova! Your case is interesting! Did you find a solution?

entry.title: Build Apps Preview Editable not working
entry.content: Hi, i noticed since last night, when i preview an app in Build Apps Preview Web there is a selectable dropdown for versions including and editable selection.   On apps where i have a version created,  if select editable i will get the spinner for loading only. However, if I select a version that will preview. apps with no version created, i can get to the current version and view changes dynamically.   Is this a problem others are experiencing?

entry.title: SAP OCI catalog - support SSO integartion
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have received a request from a vendor to to set up an OCI Catalog and they would like SSO integration.

We would like to check if if SAP OCI catalog can support SSO integration if vendor is capable of supporting SSO (SAML).

Any document to guide us will be really helpful.

Looking forward to your assistance.

entry.title: Re: User Defined SRV URL
entry.content: Thanks for this. I had a customer change the space name in the dev environment and the default route of my cap service changed. This was really helpful

entry.title: Mastering Variable-Driven Currency Conversion in SAP Analytics Cloud Planning
entry.content:
Hello,
I’m Filippo Naggi, an Enterprise Architect with a deep passion for enterprise technologies, specifically SAP SAC, SAP BPC, Onestream, and BigQuery. Today, I am excited to take you through a detailed guide on how to build a variable-driven measure for currency conversion within SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). This technique will enable you to select the desired destination currency dynamically and apply it seamlessly across various reports and analyses within your model.

Currency conversion is a critical aspect of financial modeling, especially when dealing with multi-currency datasets. Having the flexibility to switch between different currencies while maintaining consistency across measures is vital for making informed business decisions. In this blog, we will cover step-by-step how to create such a solution using a variable-driven approach in SAC.

Step 1: Model Setup

First, we’ll begin by setting up the model. Our model includes two dimensions, and we start with a source measure in USD (U.S. dollars), which will be our default currency for this exercise. As shown in Fig 1.x, this setup is foundational to ensure that our measures are properly aligned with the currency variable we will be adding later.

Fig. 1.1 The Multidimensional Model

Fig. 1.2 The Source measure in USD - Measure name: Translated

 

Step 2: Activating Currency Conversion

Next, we activate the built-in feature for currency conversion within SAC. SAC provides a robust framework to handle currency conversions dynamically, and by activating this feature, we can streamline the process. In Fig 2, you’ll see how the currency conversion option is enabled within the model, making it ready to support multiple currencies.

Fig.2 Currency Conversion Model Setup

 

 

Step 3: Building a Variable for Currency Conversion

The core of our approach lies in creating a variable that drives the currency conversion. This variable allows users to select the desired target currency, which can be applied universally throughout the system. In Fig 3, I will walk you through how to build and configure this variable step-by-step.

Fig.3 Currency Variable Settings

Step 4: Currency Rate Table

Of course, the actual conversion rates must be managed, and SAC simplifies this process through the use of a currency rate table. This table stores all the exchange rates that will be used in the conversion calculations. Fig 4 shows an example of how this table is configured and how the system pulls the relevant rates for each currency conversion.

Fig 4. Rates setup

 

 

Step 5: Applying the Variable in a Story

Once the variable is ready, it’s time to integrate it into a story. In this example, we’ll build a table in the story that leverages the currency conversion variable. SAC will automatically prompt users to select the currency at runtime. As shown in Fig 5, the system can even provide a default currency, which can be changed based on the user’s preferences.

Fig.5 Variable Story Input

 

 

 

 

Step 6: Runtime Selection of Different Currencies

The beauty of this solution is the flexibility it offers at runtime. Users can easily select different currencies based on the available options in the variable. As demonstrated in Fig 6.x, users can toggle between different currency selections, and the entire dataset will automatically adjust to reflect the newly selected currency.

Fig 6.1 USD Toggle

Fig 6.2 EUR Toggle

 

 

 

Step 7: Using the Feature in the Excel Plug-In

Lastly, it’s worth noting that this functionality isn’t limited to just the web interface of SAC. The variable-driven currency conversion can also be utilized through the SAC Excel plug-in. This allows users to work with currency conversions directly within their Excel environment, providing additional flexibility for analysis and reporting. Fig 7.x shows an example of how the feature operates within the plug-in.

Fig 7.1 Variable Selector - USD

Fig 7.2 Rate: USD

Fig 7.3 Variable Selector - EUR

Fig 7.4 Rate: EUR

 

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, variable-driven currency measures are a powerful feature within SAP Analytics Cloud, providing unmatched flexibility and control over financial reporting and analysis in multi-currency environments. This functionality highlights the multidimensional capabilities of SAC, making it an ideal tool for enterprise-scale financial planning and analysis.

If you have any questions or require further assistance in implementing this feature, feel free to reach out to me directly. I’d be happy to assist you in exploring the full potential of variable-driven currency conversion in your SAC environment.

Thank you for following along and Stefano for kicking off the interest in deep diving into this.

I hope you all found this guide helpful!

Best regards,
Filippo Naggi

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: More monitors is a good thing

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: Fueling up with food, diving into code with coffees, and leveling up with games—it's all about balance at SAP Techtoberfest!

entry.title: Re: UI5 UploadSet beforeItemAdded event no longer fires.
entry.content: If you set a javascript debug in the beforeItemAdded event method then does it stop there?

entry.title: Re: Returning Excel with a CAP service
entry.content:
Hi @TheWind 
I tried it with version 8.3 and it works for me.
I added my media field as LargeBinary type in the schema, in my case with a fixed media type, but this can be made dynamic as described in the documentation:

entity Books { // ...
        attachmentXlsx : LargeBinary @Core.MediaType: 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet';
}

I see it as Edm.Stream in the metadata.

<EntityType Name="Books">
  <Key>
    <PropertyRef Name="ID"/>
  </Key>
  <Property Name="ID" Type="Edm.Int32" Nullable="false"/>
  <Property Name="attachmentXlsx" Type="Edm.Stream"/>
</EntityType>

I used the custom read logic as follows, I used the createReadStream from Node.js fs to read a static file:

  srv.on('READ', 'Books', (req, next) => {
    if (attachmentIsRequested(req)) {
      const readable = fs.createReadStream('files/test.xlsx');
      return {
        value: readable,
        $mediaContentType: 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet',
        $mediaContentDispositionFilename: 'test.xlsx', // > optional
        $mediaContentDispositionType: 'inline' // > optional
      }
    }
    return next()
  });

By typing the following URL into my browser, I was able to download and open the Excel file, and the content was correct (according to the ID, this should point to an existing record):

http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/galactic/Books(201)/image

It might be worth trying a direct browser link before the UI download link so that it doesn't interfere with the testing.

entry.title: CJI3 - project actual costs and CJI5 project commitment line items reports
entry.content:
Where can I find documentation that explains the conditions that have to be met for transactions to be displayed in each of these reports.  I'm looking for detailed, in-depth documentation related to these reports so I can explain to end users the characteristics of transactions displayed in the respective reports (including their definitions of "actuals" and "commitments").

I'm also looking for the logic behind S_ALR_87013542 and the only applicable doc I can find that explains it refers to the differences between this report and CJI3.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My name is Thompson Igunma, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I hold a Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering and am currently a PhD student in Materials Science and Engineering. I have 11 years of experience in production, manufacturing, and quality control on the shop floor.

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in SAP Manufacturing, Production Planning, and Materials Management.

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because I believe it will enhance my skills and open up new opportunities in my field.

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in technology and business transformation in the manufacturing sector.

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

Best regards,

Thompson Igunma

Best regards,

Thompson Igunma

entry.title: missing security patches
entry.content:
We haven’t patched our SAP ABAP system since 2017. Can The System Recommendations (SysRec) application within the Solution Manager discover, analyze and apply all the missing security patches to our SAP ABAP system without necessarily applying Support Package Stacks?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: missing security patches
entry.content:
Dear *,

 

Thank you for contacting SAP Support.

 

Please allow me to answer your question.

Firstly, the relevant configuration of System Recommendations needs to be completed in Solution Manager System.

Then, System Recommendations can discover and analyze all the missing security patches, and display them in System Recommendations application.

System Recommendations can not apply all the missing security patches, because System Recommendations can only help to discover and display security patches that may be applicable at the moment, in fact, they may not all be applicable to your system, so you need to check them one by one before applying them to the relevant system.

 

Further info, please check KBA 2554633.

##2554633 - SysRec: System Recommendations Configuration Guide - SAP Solution Manager 7.2

---------

More information can be found on the official page System Recommendations > Resource tab > Usage, configuration and customizing of System Recommendations (SAP Solution Manager 7.2).

---------

 

Best regards,

Leo

SAP Support

entry.title: How to change in house production duration
entry.content:
Hi Experts!

We have a question about in house production duration. Why our in house production duration and processing time is set to 999 days? on material master data we already set the in house production to 0. And for this case , we have been trying to use non material stock for the routing so the field for in house production in app Change Material is unavailable. But why the in house production duration and processing time is set to 999 days?

Any suggestion what we have to check and change?

Thank You!

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Merge feature branch to MAIN branch in gCTS fiori app
entry.content:
Hi,

I have implemented gCTS in S/4 HANA 2023 with a remote GitHub repository. Task based commit is enabled by implementing BADI. There is a MAIN branch in the repository. In SAP gCTS Fiori app, Developer 1 created BRANCH1 and Developer 2 created BRANCH2 in the same repository in their gCTS fiori app logins. when they started working on the same existing Z program. Automatic PUSH is switched OFF. When they released the tasks, changes are committed to the respective  BRANCH1 and BRANCH2. What is the approach to merge both BRANCH1 and BRANCH2 code into the MAIN branch in gCTS fiori app?

In opensap course, below approach is mentioned:

- In Developer's gCTS Fiori app : Developer to merge MAIN branch to the respective BRANCH and push the new commit to REMOTE repository. And then developer to login to GitHub remote repository, do the COMPARE & PULL, MERGE etc in REMOTE GitHub repository as a collaborator. And then click on 'Update to latest commit' in gCTS fiori app?    

Everytime developer needs to login to Github remote repository and and do the COMPARE & PULL, MERGE etc to have up to date 'current active commit' of MAIN branch? Can't we do it directly in gCTS Fiori app? Like developer 1 releases the task and open gCTS app, under branch tab, merge the BRANCH1 to MAIN branch and developer 2 also do the same? and finally push the latest local commit to remote GitHub repository?

Can you please help me understand this?

-Sunil

entry.title: Re: Setting up Functional Currency for a Starter Pack Country
entry.content: I agree, this is misleading 'coz there's a separate Functional Currency field in FINSC_LEDGER. The one described here is Free Defined Currency.

https://me.sap.com/notes/1581695

Note 1680803 - SYB: SAP Adaptive Server Enterprise - Best Practice for SAP Business Suite and SAP BW
https://me.sap.com/notes/1680803

entry.content:

There is nothing wrong with the set up of invoicing plan as well as the Withholding Tax maintained in the vendor level.

Additionally, first IR was posted correctly automatically but the mentioned issue occurs on the next monthly auto-postings. What could be the possible root cause of this issue?

#invoicingplan #wht #withholdingtax

entry.title: New Member
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My name is Virmary, you can call me V, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I’m a project management professional with experience in customer success, with a focus on strategic planning, process improvement and team-building. 

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in Project management and supply chain optimization within SAP.

I’m pursuing this certification as part of my journey to transition into a new career focused on technology and business transformation. I see SAP as a key tool in enabling organizations to optimize operations and innovate, and I'm excited to see where this path will take me. 

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in AI and business transformation, I’m eager to explore how emerging technologies can complement SAP systems to streamline processes and enhance organizational efficiency. 

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

Best regards,  

Virmary Acosta

entry.title: Seeking Clarification on Clean Core Strategy for New ABAP Custom Code on BTP ABAP vs ECC
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently involved in a strategic decision and would greatly value your insights. Management is advocating for all new custom ABAP developments to be built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), rather than directly in ECC, as part of a "clean core" approach, as we have an upcoming Migration to S/4 HANA Private Cloud.

However, I am struggling to fully understand how developing on BTP ABAP achieves the clean core objective compared to traditional ECC development. Here are my concerns:

  1. Custom Code in Both Environments: Whether we develop on BTP ABAP or in ECC, the main custom code logic still needs to be in ECC application layer. Wouldn’t this result in similar technical debt and maintenance challenges, even if the code resides in BTP?

  2. True Clean Core: From my understanding, the essence of clean core means minimal customizations and decoupled extensions. However, developing custom ABAP code on BTP appears to shift the code off ECC, but most of the application logic would still be written as custom OData services in ECC. Is this really achieving the intended goal of a clean core?

  3. Future S/4HANA Migration: With the upcoming migration to S/4HANA, I’m concerned about the additional overhead required to refactor custom code that was developed in BTP ABAP for ECC. Wouldn’t it be easier to develop and refactor code directly in ECC until after the migration? Post-migration, BTP ABAP could work more seamlessly with the new S/4HANA data model.

I’d really appreciate your insights on whether developing all new custom ABAP code on BTP ABAP in this pre-S/4HANA phase is in line with SAP’s clean core strategy, or if there's a more practical approach to minimize rework and technical debt.

Thank you in advance for your advice!
Best regards,
Anirudh

entry.title: Re: Shipping condition unexpectedly being changed in VT01N
entry.content: You may want to check the copy routine assigned to the shipment type.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Data Modeling with the latest features of ABAP Core Data Services
entry.content: Very engaging session Matthias Herchenroether and Katharina Altrichter Thank you. Good one.

entry.title: My Intro
entry.content:
Good Evening,

My name is Keron Garraway, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I've spent 7 years working in warehouse management, shipping and receiving, and inventory management. For the past 6 years, I’ve been running my own home inspection business, where I've inspected everything from 800 sq. ft. condos in Midtown Atlanta to 12,000 sq. ft. mixed-use commercial buildings. I even developed my own home inspection course and have trained nearly a dozen new inspectors. 

 

Throughout this journey, I’ve built strong relationships within both the tech and real estate industries, and I've gained a lot of valuable knowledge. Now, I’m excited to take on a new challenge and transition fully into the tech space, specifically exploring how my experiences can contribute to the ERP world, particularly SAP. I’m eager to see how my skills can add value here and learn from the community as I embark on this new chapter.

 

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in the ERP space, bringing a diverse background that began as a forklift driver and progressed through multiple leadership roles. I’ve always had a passion for technology, constantly pushing systems to see how I could make them better or spot potential issues. Whether it was speeding through processes or finding loopholes to make my job more efficient, this drive to optimize became part of my daily workflow without me even realizing it. Looking back, I believe this mindset is what set me apart from my peers and helped me progress in the industry. It's that curiosity and problem-solving approach that I’m excited to bring into the ERP space as I transition into supply chain management. I'm eager to leverage these skills in the ERP world, particularly to streamline and optimize supply chain processes using innovative technologies. I look forward to contributing to this space and growing within it!

 

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in extended shipping, supply chain and AI

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

 

Best regards,  

Keron Garraway

Your Future SAP CoWorker…

entry.title: SAP Fiori Elements Object Page Table: Weird row selection behavior
entry.content:
Hello,
I have a RAP application which consists of List Report and Object Page with table on it. Multi selection feature is on. I have bounded action on the table. There is something weird happening with selections, hope you could help me.

1. Selected Rows are lost after sorting, filtering
If i select first two rows and sort the table, the first two rows remain selected. Because of sorting it is different data, however.
Before sorting

After sorting

If I use table of type Responsive Table, the behaviour is correct. The original rows remain selected but their position it table changes correctly because of sorting.
Questions: Is this a bug or feature? Is it possible to influence the behavior? Clearing the selection after filtering/sorting would help a bit but I was not able to make it happen (I read about RememberSelections option in other posts)

2. Selected rows are retained after calling the bound action
I select a few rows and push the action button. The action is performed on the backend, data is updated in the table, but the rows remain selected, which can be and in my case really is very confusing for the end user.
Questions: Is it possible to control the row selection from RAP backend (binding the model property to selected, some annotation)? Is it possible to switch the feature off?

Thank you,
Zdenek

entry.title: Re: Formatted Search PO Screen

Hi,

Try this

select "U_Print_Rev" from OCRD where "CardCode"=$[$4.0.0]

Hope this helps,

SonTran

entry.content:
Not possible to determine shipping data for material R010050

Message no. 06280

entry.title: Re: Customer field Not shown in ME21n and ME22N
entry.content: urgent req

entry.title: What’s new with Joule in the upcoming SAP SuccessFactors 2H 2024 release
entry.content:
Joule recently celebrated its first birthday and it’s amazing how much it has grown since being introduced just one year ago. Over the last 12 months, SAP has continuously enhanced Joule’s copilot capabilities with innovations that improve the conversational experience and increase the value the solution can bring to our customers. Many of the enhancements delivered so far and many more on the solution roadmap are the result of some specific end-user expectations of what a copilot should deliver:

  1. People expect Joule to deliver a more “ChatGPT-like” experience
  2. People expect a smoother conversational experience from a copilot
  3. People are eager to talk to Joule in additional languages beyond English

Let’s review some of the ways Joule is growing to meet these expectations from our SAP SuccessFactors community in our upcoming release.

Enhanced conversational experience and new Joule use cases

In this release, we are introducing Joule Functions, a new conversational experience that enables smarter, more adaptive conversations by managing dialog flows, improving language understanding, and keeping track of chat history and user context for seamless interactions. What can users expect from this new conversational experience?

  • Joule users will enjoy more natural, human-like conversations with more accurate intent recognition.
  • Users can now more easily switch between topics while keeping chat history and context intact.
  • Joule will provide more precise answers tailored to your previous interactions and context.

For example, as shown in the images below, if a user asks to generate feedback to send to an employee, Joule will retain the context of the conversation, allowing the user to request additional updates to the generated content before it is sent. Rather than following predefined paths, Joule responds even more intelligently to the user’s needs, offering a fluid and natural conversational experience.

 

 Figure 1 – An example of Joule’s new enhanced conversational experience

The following new transactional use cases are included in this release and take advantage of the new conversational experience:

  • AI-assisted onboarding task completion - Helps improve the efficiency of HR Partners and Managers by completing onboarding tasks via Joule.
  • View performance and development goals - Employees can now efficiently access a list of performance and development goals, as well as review goal details, by using conversational chat in Joule.

 Figure 2 – An example of AI-assisted onboarding task completion via Joule

Additional new navigational use cases that take advantage of the new conversational experience are included in this release, including the ability to assist new hires during onboarding as well as the ability to view information from Opportunity Marketplace, Growth Portfolio, and job requisitions.

All of the aforementioned new Joule use cases will be available with the production release on November 15, 2024. After that date, we will resume delivering new Joule features and use cases on a monthly basis and we will also continue to issue the associated What’s New Viewer (WNV) documents each month. It’s important to note that the WNV will not reflect these new items until November 15.

A total of 70 existing Joule use cases are being upgraded to take advantage of the new conversational experience. Please see the Appendix in this blog for a complete list of the Joule use cases that are being upgraded in conjunction with the 2H 2024 release. All use cases (whether transactional or navigational) not upgraded in this release will be upgraded in the monthly Joule releases beginning in December 2024.

New Joule data center strategy

To make it easier for customers to adopt Joule, we have enhanced the Joule data center consumption strategy. This change introduces flexibility by allowing customers to determine which Joule data center they wish to use. In addition, this change supports global customers or those with complex landscapes who choose to integrate Joule with multiple SAP business applications on different data centers, even across different regions. SAP will continue to introduce new Joule data centers in other regions and will always consider unique customer migration needs.

Please refer to the Joule product document Data Centers Supported by Joule to understand the Joule data center details as well as the instruction on how to determine a Joule data center.

An update on Joule language availability

Four critical languages – German, Spanish, French, and Portuguese – will be supported in Joule beginning in early 2025. Several other additional languages like Japanese, Korean, Dutch, and Chinese are planned to follow in the Joule roadmap (delivery timelines not available as of this writing).

Summary

As mentioned above, it is important to remember that all new Joule features and use cases will be available to customers beginning with the 2H production release on November 15, 2024. This release brings an innovative new conversational experience to Joule, as well as several new Joule use cases, a new Joule data center strategy, and updates to the ongoing language rollout for Joule.

Finally, some SAP SuccessFactors Joule content such as access to profile, feedback, approvals, and HR policy questions will also be accessible via the SAP Mobile Start app beginning in late November, 2024. More details on that to come!

Appendix

List of 70 Joule use cases upgraded to the new conversational experience in 2H 2024 release:

  • Compensation  |  View award balance
  • Compensation  |  View compensation forms
  • Employee Central  |  View addresses

entry.title: Re: Customer field Not shown in ME21n and ME22N
entry.content: ME21N level

entry.title: Re: Sap gui progress indicator on sap module pool screen
entry.content:
Are you sure your program reaches the function module call?

Write a short demo program to see how the call of the function module works.

REPORT ytmp.

CALL FUNCTION 'SAPGUI_PROGRESS_INDICATOR'
  EXPORTING
    percentage = 30
    text       = 'Test progress indicator'.

wait UP TO 10 SECONDS.

MESSAGE 'Test status message' TYPE 'S'.

entry.title: For STO POin S4, in Me21n,Not possible to determine shipping data for material XXXX- Error Message
entry.content:
Customer 2101 does not exist (please change entry in plant XXX)-Warning Message

Not possible to determine shipping data for material XXXX- Error Message

Kindly help all configurations done

entry.title: Re: Getting 502 error on SAP MDK Web Application
entry.content:
Hi @bill_froelich,

Connectivity in Mobile Service using forward authentication, and set URL and all details in BTP Destination. For further information, I followed this blog.

Best Regards,

Madhav

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: A glimpse of my workplace and my favorite editor where I spend lot of my time currently.

entry.title: Re: Inspection lot not generating for material and Storag...
entry.content:
Hi Herman,

Thanks for your response.

According to your suggestion 

1. "Post to inspection stock" Indicator in place for material in Material master.
2. Checked quality level, There is no skip lot stage because DMR is not assign

The issue is when we post material against the STO (GRN) in with specific storage location that time it is moving stock to unrestricted. Others storage location correctly moving into quality stock.

Akash

entry.title: Re: Exploring SAP support portals & processes
entry.content: Hi Karoline.

entry.title: Re: Exploring SAP support portals & processes
entry.content: Hi Karoline. It is nice to hear from you. I would like to know more about SAP Learning Hub. Can you give me some more information please?

entry.title: New Member - Introduction
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My name is Ademola Adesokan, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I have a strong background as an SAP Associate Consultant with hands-on experience in SAP, particularly in SAP Basis, system administration, and upgrades. I've worked in industries like oil and gas, where I was responsible for managing and optimizing SAP environments to enhance business processes. Additionally, I hold several SAP certifications, including System Administration (HANA) with SAP NetWeaver 7.5, Business Process Integration with SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4 HANA Conversion and SAP System Upgrade, and SAP Activate Project Manager. In addition, I am also vast in using machine learning/data science approaches to solve problems.

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m looking forward to expanding my knowledge in SAP solutions, especially in business process integration and system optimization.

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because it aligns with my passion for leveraging SAP technologies to solve complex business challenges and drive innovation in enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems. Gaining deeper expertise in SAP S/4HANA Cloud will enable me to further enhance my ability to deliver efficient, scalable solutions for business transformation.

I’m also open to connect with others who are passionate about technology, SAP innovations, and ERP systems, particularly in areas such as business transformation, process improvement, AI, and industries like oil and gas, among many others.

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

Best regards,
Ademola Adesokan
